title: Review Order for Natural Health Product Applications - Canada.ca
content:
## Review Order for Natural Health Product Applications
Natural Health Product Licensing Bulletin No. 9, March 11, 2026
- The Natural and Non-prescription Health Products Directorate (NNHPD) is managing a high volume of applications.
- Natural Health Product (NHP) licence applications are being assigned for review based on when prioritization was requested, and not on the original submission date.
- For applications submitted before December 15, 2025, the review order will be based on the date the Workload Management Form was submitted.
- For applications received on or after December 15, 2025, the review order will be based on the application's date of receipt.
- This approach builds on previous NHP licensing bulletins and the Natural Health Products Management of Applications Policy (NHP MAP) eligibility criteria, and helps ensure a consistent and transparent review process.
This bulletin provides information on how NNHPD will assign NHP product licence applications (PLAs) for review under the workload management approach.
As outlined in previous bulletins , NNHPD implemented a workload management approach to help ensure timely and predictable processing of PLAs that will benefit Canadians. Under this approach, applicants were initially asked to submit a Workload Management Form confirming that their product would be sold or manufactured in Canada within a defined timeframe.
On December 15, 2025, the attestation questions from the Workload Management Form were integrated directly into the PLA form, streamlining the submission process for all application classes.
The volume of prioritized applications awaiting review now exceeds NNHPD's review capacity. To manage this demand, and maintain fairness and transparency, NNHPD is formalizing how applications are ordered and assigned for review.

## Implementation: Assignment of Applications for Review

### Applications submitted before December 15, 2025
For PLAs received prior to, the prioritization date is the date the Workload Management Form was submitted.
Applications will be assigned for review based on:
- the earliest prioritization date; and
- where multiple applications share the same prioritization date, the original date of application receipt.
This approach reflects when applicants established eligibility under the workload management approach and ensures consistent ordering of applications.

### Applications received on or after December 15, 2025
For applications received on or after December 15, 2025, the prioritization date is the date of receipt and will be assigned for review based on that date.

### Important Reminder on Attestations
Applicants must respond to attestation questions in the PLA form to confirm a submission's eligibility. Applicants are reminded that attestations must meet the requirements set out in the " Attestation requirements for submission " section of the NHP MAP. This includes ensuring that information provided is accurate and complete.
For inquiries about this bulletin, please contact us by email: nnhpd-dpsnso@hc-sc.gc.ca .
